title: draw.io
description: Self-hosted draw.io (diagrams.net), a client-side editor for flowcharts, UML, ER, network and other diagrams.
installation: draw.io is a stateless, client-side diagram editor served by Tomcat. Diagrams are never stored on the server: they live in the browser, on the user's local disk, or in an external storage provider the user connects (Google Drive, OneDrive, GitHub, GitLab, Dropbox), so no database add-on and no persistent volume are required. To enable those external storage integrations you must register OAuth apps with each provider and pass their client IDs via the DRAWIO_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID / DRAWIO_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID / DRAWIO_GITLAB_CLIENT_ID / DRAWIO_ONEDRIVE_CLIENT_ID env vars (left blank by default). PUBLIC_DNS / DRAWIO_BASE_URL are pre-set to the app URL so generated share/embed links are correct; update them if you change the hostname. For PNG/SVG/PDF server-side export, deploy the optional jgraph/export-server companion container and point DRAWIO_EXPORT_URL at its in-cluster service name (export then works client-side without it). No SMTP, S3 or search engine is needed.
